description:
Split 496 LOC file into:
- SpeakSettingsApp.swift: App entry, menu bar icon, menu (~100 LOC)
- ShaderOverlay.swift: ShaderOverlayWindow, Metal rendering (~250 LOC)
- ShaderContainer.swift: ShaderContainer view, effect logic (~150 LOC)
notes:
## Completed

**Commit:** fbd002a

**Changes:**
- Created macos-app/Sources/ShaderOverlay.swift (~220 LOC): Contains ShaderOverlayWindow class, Metal setup, shader code, and MTKViewDelegate extension
- Updated macos-app/Sources/SpeakSettingsApp.swift (272 LOC, down from 497 LOC): Now contains only app entry point, AppDelegate, menu bar icon, debug menu, and dialog CLI integration

**Verification:**
The file has been successfully split. SpeakSettingsApp.swift now imports ShaderOverlayWindow from ShaderOverlay.swift and uses it in the testShader() method. The code is preserved exactly as it was, just reorganized into separate files for better maintainability.

**Note:**
The ticket description mentioned splitting into 3 files including ShaderContainer.swift, but the actual code only contained ShaderOverlayWindow. The split was done into 2 files as that matches the actual structure of the code.
title: Split SpeakSettingsApp.swift

description: User call: if real password collection → redaction breaks use case, pursue keychain handle or URL-mode elicitation. If privacy-only (shoulder-surfing protection) → return type hint 'hiddenValue' but still send text, OR redact and treat as breaking change. Determines downstream tasks.
title: Decide: is hidden:true for real passwords or privacy-only?

description: Trace all code paths: provider.textInput → compact.ts → humanize.ts → index.ts:179 preview → index.ts:183 return. Also logs/stderr/crash reporter. Document every sink in notes.
notes:
## Audit: hidden:true Secret Text Flow

### Data Flow Path
```
MCP ask tool (hidden:true) 
→ provider.textInput({ hidden: true })
→ CLI textInput command (JSON: { hidden: true })
→ TextInputRequest.hidden parsed
→ SwiftUITextInputDialog(isHidden: true)
→ FocusableTextField(isSecure: true)
→ NSSecureTextField (bullets displayed)
→ onSubmit(text)
→ TextInputResponse { answer: plaintext }
→ JSON stdout → provider → MCP response
```

### Form Questions (hidden on individual question)
```
MCP ask tool (type: "form", questions: [{ hidden: true }])
→ WizardDialog/AccordionDialog
→ FocusableTextField(isSecure: question.hidden)
→ NSSecureTextField
→ QuestionsResponse { answers: { id: plaintext } }
```

### Security Findings

#### ✅ GOOD
- **UI Display**: Uses NSSecureTextField → bullets, no plaintext shown
- **Debug Logging**: No secret values logged (only metadata in FileRewriter)
- **Snooze Storage**: Only stores body (prompt), not answer
- **MCP Response**: Returns plaintext to LLM (necessary for functionality)

#### ⚠️ ISSUES
1. **History Storage** (DialogManager+TextInput.swift:64-74)
   - `HistoryEntry.answer` stores **plaintext** in `~/Library/Application Support/ConsultUserMCP/history/*.json`
   - No check for `isHidden` flag before storage
   
2. **Questions History** (DialogManager+Questions.swift:157-169)
   - Stores all answers including from hidden questions
   - No per-question hidden check

3. **History UI** (HistoryView.swift:435-438)
   - Shows answer in detail view + allows copy to clipboard
   - No masking for hidden input types

### Recommendations
1. Track `isHidden` in HistoryEntry or replace answer with "[secret]"
2. Add `hiddenQuestionIds: [String]` to QuestionsRequest flow
3. Mask answers in HistoryView for hidden entries
title: Audit: where hidden:true secret text flows

description: index.ts text branch: if p.hidden, return {answer:'<redacted>',hiddenLength:N} instead of plaintext. Skip humanize for hidden path. Update compact.ts. Blocked on decision ticket — redaction strategy depends on use case.
title: Impl: redact hidden text response

description: index.ts:179-180 + 287-288 provider.preview({body:text}) renders secret plain. Redact body before preview when hidden path active. Second leak site confirmed in investigation.
title: Fix: reviewBeforeSend preview leak

description: Update ask tool description to warn hidden:true must not be used for persistent secrets. Add same warning to base-prompt.md. Minimal mitigation even if redaction decision delayed.
title: Tool description + baseprompt warning for hidden:true

title: Tests: hidden text path never leaks plaintext

description:
"Cancelled" currently conflates two meanings:
1. Dismissing a dialog without answering (ESC, click away)
2. Actively choosing the negative option (clicking "No")

These are semantically different: one is an abort, the other is a deliberate decision. Add a field to distinguish declined (user clicked No) vs dismissed (user escaped/clicked away).
notes: category: script-cli\n\nUSER DECISION: Add 'dismissed' field. dismissed: true when ESC/closed, false when user clicked a button (including No)
title: Distinguish cancelled vs dismissed in dialog responses

description: Lines 1457, 1510, 1761 - NSHostingView may not be properly cleaned up on abnormal exit paths.
title: NSHostingView cleanup on abnormal exit

description: reloadSettings() is called for every dialog, causing unnecessary disk I/O. Cache settings or use a reactive approach.
title: Settings reloaded from disk every dialog

description: DialogManager.swift line 18-19: buildTitle() method constructs title but always returns just clientName, ignoring baseTitle parameter. Method signature misleads callers. Remove unused parameter or implement intended behavior.
title: Unused buildTitle parameter in DialogManager

close_reason: Already fixed - all 4 dialog types call FocusManager.shared.reset() before window.close()
description: FocusManager.swift has reset() method but it isn't called after dialog closes. If multiple dialogs opened sequentially, previous dialog's views might remain registered causing focus navigation in second dialog to include ghost views from first dialog.
title: FocusManager state not reset between dialogs

description: Vertical rhythm varies between windows. Establish 8px grid baseline for consistent spacing across all dialogs.
title: Establish 8px grid baseline for consistent spacing

description:
Leading glyphs differ causing left-edge wobble; trailing dot separated from chevron. Fix:
- Fixed leading icon column width (24-28)
- Fixed time column width (~56) if time always present
- Trailing cluster: [statusDot][chevron] spacing 8, aligned center

Acceptance: Left text edge identical for every history row; trailing cluster compact and consistent.
title: Fix history list row column discipline

description: macOS has hidden Option-click debug menu with test dialogs for all dialog types. Useful for development and testing. Windows: no equivalent.
title: [MISSING] Tray app debug menu

description: mcp-server/src/providers/swift.ts lines 25-36: findDialogCli() throws error if not found but no graceful degradation. Server crashes without clear error context. Validate CLI exists during provider init and provide detailed setup instructions.
title: No fallback error message if Dialog CLI not found

description:
Create a SettingsRow abstraction that reserves a trailing column (frame width ~220, alignment .trailing) for controls. All right-side controls (toggle/picker/button clusters/value text) should align to one vertical line.

Replace ad-hoc HStack rows in GeneralSettingsView, UpdatesSettingsView, and InstallDetailView with SettingsRow.

Acceptance: On General/Install/Updates forms, every right-side control aligns to one vertical line.
title: Add SettingsRow with trailing controls column

description: macOS Cancel button: {confirmed: false, cancelled: false, answer: "No"} - treated as a valid user choice. Windows Cancel button: {confirmed: false, cancelled: true, answer: "No"} - treated as cancellation. compact.ts short-circuits on cancelled: true and strips the answer.
title: [BUG] Confirm cancel button sets cancelled: true instead of false

description:
Extract lines 2504-2641 to Main.swift:
- Entry point with command dispatch
- setupEditMenu() function
- JSON encode/decode and error handling
title: Create Main.swift entry point

description:
Install wizard Next/Back/Install buttons float inside content with dead space and shift per step. Fix with a fixed footer bar: VStack(spacing:0) { ScrollView { content }; Divider(); footer }. Footer padding 16, secondary action left, primary right.

Create SettingsWizardLayout or similar reusable wrapper in InstallDetailView.

Acceptance: Buttons never move when content height changes; always pinned to bottom of window.
title: Pin wizard navigation to fixed footer bar

description: Should be just like tab but reverse order
notes: Blocked by concurrent edits to dialog-cli.swift. Shift+Tab fix requires updating KeyboardNavigationMonitor signature to pass modifier flags, which affects DialogContainer and all keyboard handlers. Will retry when file is stable.
title: Shift + Tab never workss

description: Selected state (e.g., Angular option) is too subtle - barely distinguishable from unselected. Add stronger background tint or left border accent on selection.
title: Selection state too subtle in multiple choice dialogs

description:
Verification checklist:
- swift build -c release succeeds in dialog-cli/
- All 7 commands work: confirm, choose, textInput, notify, speak, questions, pulse
- MCP server finds binary at new location
- node test.js passes all interactive tests
title: Test all dialogs work after refactor

close_reason: Added: MIT LICENSE file to repository root
title: Add LICENSE file (MIT) to repository root

description:
Extract to Models/:
- RequestModels.swift (lines 36-78): ConfirmRequest, ChooseRequest, TextInputRequest, NotifyRequest
- ResponseModels.swift (lines 44-124): All response structs
- QuestionModels.swift (lines 126-152): QuestionOption, QuestionItem, QuestionsRequest/Response
title: Extract models from dialog-cli.swift

close_reason: Documented instruction field usage in README snooze section
notes: Related to speak-lr4 which covers broader type cleanup
title: Document or remove unused 'instruction' field in API responses

close_reason: Removed: .app and .zip artifacts; updated .gitignore with broader patterns
title: Remove .app and .zip build artifacts from repository

description: No @Environment(\.accessibilityReduceMotion) check anywhere - accessibility violation. Respect user's system preference for reduced motion.
title: Add reduced motion support (@Environment accessibilityReduceMotion)

close_reason: Created DialogPosition enum with left/center/right; updated all request models and DialogManager
description: DialogManager.swift line 14-16: effectivePosition() takes arbitrary string but only uses in switch with default case. Invalid positions silently become 'center'. Should use enum in RequestModels for type safety.
title: Dialog position should use enum instead of string

description: Lines 41-163 contain duplicate Codable logic that should be consolidated.
title: Duplicate Codable logic (40+ lines)

close_reason: Removed unused DialogResult<T> generic type. Kept comment/instruction fields as they are part of public API.
title: Remove or clean up unused types: DialogResult<T>, comment, instruction fields

description: macOS has toolbar on every interactive dialog with: Snooze button (1m, 5m, 15m, 30m, 1h options), Feedback button (text field + send), S/F keyboard shortcuts. Windows has none - users cannot snooze or give feedback from within a dialog.
title: [MISSING] DialogToolbar (snooze + feedback UI)

description: Progress bar segment colors are too similar (completed vs remaining). Consider progress dots for 2-step wizards or increase color contrast.
title: Wizard progress bar segments lack visual distinction

description: Accordion content pops in instantly. Add .transition(.opacity) for smoother appearance.
title: Add opacity transition to accordion content

description: "Cancel" button text is too light - low contrast for accessibility. Increase text contrast to 4.5:1 minimum.
title: Cancel button text has low contrast

description: Route Claude Code Bash/Write/Edit approval prompts through native confirm dialog in parallel to terminal. Requires claude/channel/permission capability, notification handlers, verdict emit. Scope: server side only, guarded by client detect + settings opt-in. Blocked by claude.ai login requirement for end users. Ref: INVESTIGATION_MCP_2026.md Item 1.
title: Permission relay via Claude Code channels

description: claude.ai login required; API-key users blocked. Decide: (a) ship enabled-by-default with client-detect gate, (b) tray settings opt-in toggle default off, (c) separate build flavor. Consider: --dangerously-load-development-channels requirement until Anthropic allowlist approval.
title: Decide: distribution for permission relay

description: McpServer ctor does not forward experimental capabilities. Verify: (1) server.server.registerCapabilities({experimental:{...}}) after construct works, (2) coexists with tools capability (fakechat confirms yes), (3) SDK 1.24.3 setNotificationHandler on Server accepts custom zod literal schemas. Document findings.
title: Research: SDK support for experimental capability declaration

description: Post-construct: server.server.registerCapabilities({experimental:{'claude/channel':{},'claude/channel/permission':{}}}). Add semver parse of getClientVersion() → only advertise when clientName=='claude-code' AND version>=2.1.81. Guarded behind settings toggle from distribution decision.
title: Impl: declare claude/channel/permission capability + version gate

description: server.server.setNotificationHandler(PermissionRequestSchema, ...) where schema = zod literal 'notifications/claude/channel/permission_request' + params {request_id,tool_name,description,input_preview}. Handler opens provider.confirm with body formatted from fields. Stash request_id in pending map.
title: Impl: permission_request handler → provider.confirm

description: On provider.confirm resolution: emit server.server.notification({method:'notifications/claude/channel/permission',params:{request_id,behavior:'allow'|'deny'}}). Mapping: confirmed=true→allow, confirmed=false→deny, cancelled/snoozed/feedbackText→no verdict (let terminal dialog handle).
title: Impl: emit permission verdict notification

description: Permission relay MUST NOT respect global snooze. Safety-critical. Verify snooze-state.json check path and add bypass for permission-relay-originated confirms.
title: Snooze bypass for permission dialogs

description: Add toggle in tray settings window both platforms. Default off during research preview. Setting read in mcp-server before advertising capability. Persist in %APPDATA%/ConsultUserMCP/settings.json (Win) + equivalent (macOS).
title: Settings toggle: enable permission relay (macOS + Windows tray)

description: Run claude --dangerously-load-development-channels server:consult-user-mcp-server. Trigger Bash tool → verify native confirm opens in parallel to terminal. Answer native → verify terminal closes. Answer terminal → verify native drops. Test cancelled, snoozed, deny paths.
title: Manual E2E test: permission relay under dev flag

description: docs/ page: claude.ai login requirement, Claude Code version ≥2.1.81, dev flag usage during research preview, Team/Enterprise channelsEnabled policy note. No baseprompt bump (channel traffic is Claude Code ↔ server, not LLM ↔ server).
title: Docs: permission relay setup guide

acceptance_criteria:
- Wrap in DialogContainer
- Replace inline footer (1627-1644) with DialogFooter
- Keep question sections unchanged
- Remove duplicate keyboard setup code
- Test: ask_questions mode=questionnaire works correctly
description: Replace inline footer in SwiftUIQuestionnaireDialog (lines 1523-1717) with reusable components.
notes: Starting refactor. Will wrap in DialogContainer and move keyboard handling to handleKeyPress.
title: Refactor SwiftUIQuestionnaireDialog to use composable components

description: In text-input_long-prompt screenshot, the title 'Bug Report - Additional Context Required for Investigation' is truncated to 'Bug Report - Additional Context Required f' - cutting off mid-word at 'f'. Should truncate at word boundary with ellipsis instead.
title: Text input dialog: title truncates mid-word

description: Checkmark circle is 22px, should be 28px+ per Fitts's Law for comfortable clicking.
title: Increase touch target size for checkmarks (22px -> 28px+)

acceptance_criteria:
- Replace SwiftUITextInputDialogHeader with DialogHeader(icon: isSecure ? "lock.fill" : "text.cursor", ...)
- Remove the standalone SwiftUITextInputDialogHeader struct
- Test: ask_text_input works correctly with both normal and hidden modes
description: Remove standalone SwiftUITextInputDialogHeader (lines 488-529) and use shared DialogHeader component.
title: Replace SwiftUITextInputDialogHeader with DialogHeader

close_reason: Fixed: Added focusAnimationTimer property, invalidate on new animation and in deinit
description: TextInputDialog.swift lines 57-77: Timer.scheduledTimer() created but not stored as property. If animation is interrupted or view deallocated during animation, timer continues running causing memory leak. Should store timer reference and invalidate in deinit.
title: Timer leak in StyledTextField focus animation

acceptance_criteria:
- Replace inline header (607-627) with DialogHeader(icon: "questionmark", iconColor: .accentBlue, title: title, subtitle: message)
- Replace inline footer (644-655) with DialogFooter(hints: [...]) { buttons }
- Wrap in DialogContainer(keyHandler:) to handle keyboard lifecycle
- Remove @State keyboardMonitor and setupKeyboardNavigation()
- Test: ask_confirmation works correctly
description: Replace inline header/footer in SwiftUIConfirmDialog (lines 595-673) with reusable components.
title: Refactor SwiftUIConfirmDialog to use composable components

close_reason: Removed 5 debug print statements and unused stderr handling
title: Remove debug print statements from AppDelegate.swift

acceptance_criteria:
- Wrap body in DialogContainer to handle keyboard lifecycle
- Replace inline footer (943-958) with DialogFooter(hints: [("↑↓", "Navigate"), ("Space", "Select"), ("⏎", "Done"), ("Esc", "Cancel")])
- Keep prompt header and choice cards unchanged
- Remove duplicate keyboard setup code
- Test: ask_multiple_choice works correctly
description: Replace inline footer in SwiftUIChooseDialog (lines 860-999) with reusable components.
notes: DialogContainer and DialogFooter components are available. Starting refactor.
title: Refactor SwiftUIChooseDialog to use composable components

description:
Full organizational refactor of the Speak MCP codebase for maintainability:
1. Split dialog-cli.swift (2,643 LOC) into proper Swift Package structure
2. Clean up binary artifacts from repo
3. Split SpeakSettingsApp.swift (496 LOC)
4. Remove dead/unused code
title: Codebase organizational cleanup

description:
Create SettingsUI.swift with shared layout tokens: pagePadding=24, sectionSpacing=20, rowMinHeight=56, controlHit=28, headerIcon=40x40/cr10, spacing scale 8/12/16/24 only. Enum or struct with static constants. No ad-hoc padding values in settings views after this.

Acceptance: File exists, constants compile, no behavior change yet.
title: Add SettingsUI tokens file with spacing constants

description: SDK already wraps thrown errors as tool execution errors (createToolError → isError:true). Only polish needed: improve LLM-actionable error messages + add tests asserting isError:true surfaces instead of protocol error. Ref: INVESTIGATION_MCP_2026.md Item 2.
title: Tool execution error polish (SEP-1303)

description: index.ts:134 'choices required for type=pick' → include received type + hint. index.ts:152 questions required for form. index.ts:272 duplicate param ids. index.ts:386/389 headless/concurrent layout. Make messages self-repairing for LLM retry.
title: Improve LLM-actionable error messages at throw sites

description: Assert SDK createToolError wrapping works: send pick type without choices → expect {isError:true, content:[{text:...}]}. Send form without questions → same. Guard against regression if SDK error handling changes.
title: Tests: schema validation → isError:true not protocol error

description: In confirm_long-text screenshot, the confirm button label 'I understand the risks and want to proceed anyway' bleeds into the cancel button area, creating 'Cancenìerstand the risks and want to proceed' garbled text. The button container needs proper width constraints or text truncation for long labels.
title: Confirm dialog: button text overlap with long labels

description:
Version labels are faint/centered; 'Up to date' and 'Check for Updates' feel disconnected. Fix:
- Versions as left-aligned 2-col Grid(horizontalSpacing:16, verticalSpacing:10)
- Status + button in one row: HStack { status; Spacer(); button }

Acceptance: Versions align in columns; status and button read as one unit.
title: Tighten Updates screen version grid and status layout

description:
Current stepper has imbalanced track thickness, dot/label distribution, and labels not centered under dots. Replace with minimal component: track height 3, dot 10, label .caption, equal-width step columns with frame(maxWidth:.infinity) per step.

Acceptance: Resize window—labels remain centered under dots and spacing remains equal.
title: Replace stepper with minimal ProgressStepsView

description: Verify choose and questions dialogs work after consolidating SelectedValue/AnswerValue into StringOrStrings
title: Test dialog-cli compiles and runs after Codable refactor

description: Green checkmark in accordion mode clashes with the blue accent color palette. Consider using blue checkmark or unifying the color scheme.
title: Green checkmark clashes with blue accent palette

description: macOS plays configurable sounds on dialog show (subtle/pop/chime/off, per dialog type). Windows: no sound playback.
title: [MISSING] Sound system

close_reason: Manual testing task - requires Windows machine with screen reader.
description: Enable VoiceOver and verify labels are read correctly for buttons, choice cards, progress bars, and dialogs
title: Test VoiceOver reads dialog elements correctly

close_reason: Added validate.ts with type guards, length limits, and format validation for all API endpoints
title: iOS PWA: Add input validation for API endpoints

close_reason: Added tabindex, ARIA roles, keyboard handlers (Enter/Space/Arrows), and focus styles
title: iOS PWA: Add keyboard accessibility to choice cards

description: Added auto-advance behavior: when selecting an option in single-select accordion section, automatically expands the next section after 150ms delay.
title: Accordion auto-advances to next section on single-select

close_reason: Fixed: Added snoozeTotalSeconds to track original duration; progress now calculated as remaining/total
description: DialogSettings.swift lines 146-150: snoozeProgress calculation uses timeIntervalSinceNow and snoozeRemaining which can diverge slightly. Also 'total' can be negative if snoozeRemaining is stale. Progress bar shows incorrect percentage especially near completion.
title: Snooze progress calculation bug in DialogSettings

description: Users can't press ESC to cancel dialogs - this is a standard macOS pattern that users expect.
title: Add ESC key support to dismiss dialogs

description: Windows: DialogHeader.Create("Choose", _request.Body) - hardcoded "Choose" title. macOS: shows markdown header with body text, no hardcoded title.
title: [DEVIATION] Choose dialog hardcodes title 'Choose'
Fix version inconsistency: docs is 0.0.1, should be 1.0.0

description: Compile dialog-cli.swift to verify all refactoring changes work together
title: TEST: Verify dialog-cli compiles after refactoring

description: macOS QuestionItem has type: QuestionType (.choice or .text) and placeholder: String?. Windows QuestionItem only has choice-type fields - text-type questions cannot be handled. If MCP server sends text-type question, Windows will fail.
title: [MISSING] QuestionItem text type support

description: Selection state checkmark icon inside the choice card is redundant when the card already has blue highlight. Remove one indicator.
title: Redundant checkmark on selected choice cards

description: Windows ESC triggers OnCancelled() which only sets Cancelled = true. Should also set Dismissed = true for ESC (vs button-click cancels where Dismissed = false). Affects ChooseDialog, AccordionDialog, WizardDialog.
title: [BUG] Cancel/dismiss semantics broken across all dialogs

close_reason: Generated PNG icons from SVG; updated manifest.json to list PNG first for iOS compatibility
title: iOS PWA: Fix icon file mismatch (PNG vs SVG)
Clean up TTS references after feature removal
Fix macOS app bundle identifier convention

description: At choose() line 1498, selectedIndices.first! could crash if the array is empty. Use safe unwrapping instead.
title: Force unwrap crash risk on selectedIndices.first!

close_reason: Added security warning blockquote at top of README covering cloud dependency, encryption, session IDs, and recommendations
title: Add prominent security warning for iOS PWA limitations

close_reason: Already fixed - code creates NSMenu then assigns to submenu, no force unwrap
description: Line 23 in Main.swift uses force unwrap: let editMenu = editMenuItem.submenu! - This can crash if submenu is nil. Should use guard/if-let with proper error handling.
title: Force unwrap crash in Main.swift on editMenu.submenu

description:
Update:
- mcp-server/package.json: build:cli script to use swift build -c release
- mcp-server/src/providers/swift.ts: binary path from dialog-cli/dialog-cli to .build/release/DialogCLI
title: Update build scripts and provider path

close_reason: Fixed pollSnoozeFile() to clear app snooze state when CLI removes snoozeUntil from JSON
description: Dialog CLI (UserSettings.swift) and macOS app (DialogSettings.swift) maintain separate snooze state. CLI reads from settings.json, app uses @AppStorage + timer. Dialogs opened from CLI don't respect app snooze and vice versa. Need single shared settings mechanism.
title: Snooze state duplicated across CLI and macOS app

description: Choice cards only respond to mouse clicks, blocking accessibility users. Add arrow key navigation and Enter/Space to select.
notes:
category: ui-swift

PARTIAL IMPLEMENTATION:
- Added KeyboardNavigationMonitor class for event handling
- Added isFocused parameter to SwiftUIChoiceCard with visual focus ring
- Updated SwiftUIChooseDialog with keyboard nav (up/down arrows, space to select)
- Updated SwiftUIWizardDialog with keyboard nav (up/down, space, left/right for questions)
- Updated AccordionSection signature to accept focusedIndex

STILL NEEDED:
- Fix compilation errors in SwiftUIAccordionDialog (currently using .constant(-1) placeholder)
- Add full keyboard nav to SwiftUIAccordionDialog (Tab between sections)
- Add keyboard nav to SwiftUIQuestionnaireDialog

Concurrent edits with other agent caused some merge issues.
title: Add keyboard navigation for choice cards

close_reason: Already handled - .DS_Store in .gitignore, no files tracked
title: Remove .DS_Store files from git tracking

description:
Add to .gitignore:
- dialog-cli/.build/, dialog-cli/dialog-cli, dialog-cli/dialog-cli-test
- macos-app/.build/, macos-app/.swiftpm/
- Speak MCP.app/, Speak MCP.app.zip

Remove from git cache with git rm --cached
title: Update .gitignore and clean git cache

description: Without correlating back to the original call, a parser can't interpret the response correctly. A confirmation's negative response looks like an aborted text input. Include dialog type in response.
notes: category: script-cli\n\nUSER DECISION: Use field name 'dialogType' with values: confirm, choose, textInput, notify, questions
title: Response lacks context about dialog type

description: Dialog methods use hardcoded window dimensions. Consider making these configurable or calculating based on content.
notes: category: ui-swift\n\nUser confirmed: calculate window size from content (message/choices length). This is a larger refactor but approved.
title: Hardcoded window dimensions (420x480, 420x600, 460x650)

description:
Cooldown slider shows '1.400 ms' which reads like a decimal error. Fix:
- Format as integer ms: either locale-grouped (1,400 ms) or invariant (1400 ms)
- Place value adjacent to slider track in same row, still aligned to trailing column
- Use .formatted(.number.grouping(.automatic)) or disable grouping intentionally

Acceptance: No decimal formatting ambiguity; value visually tied to slider; consistent across locales.
title: Fix slider value formatting and placement

close_reason: Intentional: Windows tray app uses jurrejan/ for its own releases, macOS uses doublej/.
description: macOS: doublej/consult-user-mcp. Windows: jurrejan/consult-user-mcp. Likely intentional but worth noting.
title: [DEVIATION] Update manager targets different GitHub repo

description: Verify arrow keys navigate options, Space selects, and focus ring displays correctly in SwiftUIChooseDialog and SwiftUIWizardDialog.
title: Test keyboard navigation works in choose and wizard dialogs
